metadata.title: niwo | Cybersecurity
metadata.description: Nikita Wokurka, Cybersecurity student and web security practitioner based in Bonn. Focused on web application security reviews, practical security guidance, and helping small teams understand their risk.

Home.heroEyebrow: Cybersecurity consulting and research for modern applications
Home.heroHeadline: Practical cybersecurity for developers and small teams
Home.heroParagraph: I help developers and small businesses understand their web application risks, fix the issues that matter most, and make better security decisions without the enterprise overhead. Based in Bonn. Currently available for focused engagements.
Home.supportLine: Web Application Security · DevSecOps · Agentic Security · Penetration Testing
Home.servicesTitle: Security support for developers and small teams
Home.servicesDescription: Focused engagements built to uncover meaningful weaknesses, explain risk clearly, and help teams fix the issues that matter first.

approach.title: An honest, practical approach to security
approach.description: I'm Nikita, a Cybersecurity student at IU International University with a background in electrical engineering and technical project coordination. I started niwo systems to combine hands-on learning with real-world practice. Most of what I know comes from building things, breaking them in controlled lab environments, and figuring out why they broke. I work best with developers and small teams who want honest, practical security support, not fear-driven upselling or reports full of jargon nobody reads.

scope.title: Focused scope
scope.text: Engagements stay centered on the flows, trust boundaries, and actions that carry real application risk.

testing.title: Realistic testing
testing.text: Testing is grounded in how modern web applications are actually attacked, not just checklist coverage.

reporting.title: Clear reporting
reporting.text: Findings are written to be understandable, defensible, and useful to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

remediation.title: Practical remediation
remediation.text: Each engagement is meant to help teams fix issues with confidence, not just collect another report.

webAppSecurity.title: Web App Security
webAppSecurity.text: I look at your web app the way an attacker would, then tell you what actually needs fixing and why.

penetrationTesting.title: Penetration Testing
penetrationTesting.text: I try to break in the same way a real attacker would, then show you exactly what worked and what to do about it.

secureDevelopment.title: Secure Development
secureDevelopment.text: I work alongside your team during development so security gets handled early, not rushed in before release.

metadata.title: Web App Security Review | niwo
metadata.description: A practical web application security review from an attacker's perspective with clear findings, plain-language explanations, and concrete steps to fix what matters.

card.title: Web App Security Review
card.text: I look at your web application from an attacker's perspective, focusing on the flows, inputs, and trust boundaries that carry real risk. You get clear findings, explained in plain language, with concrete steps to fix what matters.
card.tag: Good for: developers launching a product, small teams before a release, or anyone who wants a second pair of eyes.

hero.title: See your app the way an attacker would
hero.intro: I go through your web application from an attacker's perspective, focusing on the flows, inputs, and trust boundaries that actually carry risk. The goal isn't a scanner dump; it's a clear picture of where you're exposed and what's worth fixing first.
hero.outcomeText: Clear findings explained in plain language, ranked by what actually matters, with concrete steps your team can act on right away.

covers.title: What this covers
items: The flows that carry real risk: login, account actions, payments, anything sensitive
Authentication and session handling, and the assumptions behind them
Access control gaps: privilege escalation and broken object-level access
Input handling: injection, unsafe parsing, and weak validation
Configuration and trust-boundary issues that quietly widen your attack surface

steps.title: Understand how it's meant to work
steps.text: I start with how the app is supposed to behave, where sensitive actions happen and where trust is assumed instead of enforced.

steps.title: Probe where the risk actually is
steps.text: I focus on the flows, inputs, and edge cases that tend to hide real issues, not just what a checklist would flag.

steps.title: Write it up so you can act
steps.text: Every finding comes in plain language with its impact and a concrete fix. No vague risk scores, just what's wrong and what to do about it.

bestFor.title: Best for
items: Developers getting a product ready to launch
Small teams who want a security look before a release
Products handling user accounts, permissions, or sensitive data
Anyone who just wants an honest second pair of eyes

deliverables.title: What you get
items: A short, focused report with clear, validated findings
Plain-language explanations without jargon nobody reads
A prioritized list so you know what to fix first
Follow-up if anything in the report needs clarifying

cta.title: Want to know where your app is exposed?
cta.text: Whether you're getting ready to launch or just want a second pair of eyes, I'm happy to take a look.

metadata.title: Penetration Testing: Process & Preparation | niwo
metadata.description: Guidance to help you prepare for a penetration test: understand the process, define your scope, read the report, and ask a testing provider the right questions. Not a full pentest engagement.

card.title: Penetration Testing: Process & Preparation
card.text: Thinking about getting a pentest done but not sure where to start? I can walk you through what the process looks like, help you prepare your scope, understand what to expect from a report, and ask the right questions when talking to a testing provider.
card.tag: Note: This is not a full pentest engagement. It is practical guidance to help you get the most out of one.

hero.title: Get ready for a pentest before you pay for one
hero.intro: Thinking about getting a pentest done but not sure where to start? I can walk you through what the process actually looks like, help you prepare your scope, understand what to expect from a report, and ask the right questions when talking to a testing provider.
hero.outcomeText: A clear understanding of the process, a scope you feel confident about, and the right questions to ask, so you get real value from the test you pay for.

covers.title: What this covers
items: What a penetration test actually involves, from start to finish
Help defining and scoping what should be tested
What to expect from a report and how to read one
The right questions to ask a testing provider
How to make sure you get real value from the engagement

steps.title: Understand the process
steps.text: I walk you through what a pentest involves, so there are no surprises and no jargon you have to decode later.

steps.title: Prepare your scope
steps.text: We figure out what's worth testing and define a scope that fits your product and your budget.

steps.title: Go in informed
steps.text: You head into the engagement knowing what to expect, what to ask, and how to judge the results.

bestFor.title: Best for
items: Teams considering their first penetration test
Founders who need to meet a customer or compliance requirement
Developers who want to scope a test properly before paying for it
Anyone unsure what to expect from a testing provider

deliverables.title: What you get
items: A plain-language walkthrough of the pentest process
Help defining and documenting your scope
Guidance on reading and acting on a report
A list of the right questions to ask a provider

cta.title: Thinking about a pentest?
cta.text: If you want to go in prepared and get real value from it, let's talk through the process first.

metadata.title: Security Guidance & Consultation | niwo
metadata.description: Focused security guidance for developers and small teams with honest answers, practical advice on web security and secure development, and help prioritizing what actually matters.

card.title: Security Guidance & Consultation
card.text: Not every team needs a full pentest. Sometimes you need someone to look at your setup, answer your questions honestly, and help you prioritize. I offer focused conversations and practical advice on web security, secure development, and what to watch out for as you build.
card.tag: Good for: early-stage teams, solo developers, small businesses moving things online.

hero.title: Honest answers, without the full engagement
hero.intro: Not every team needs a full pentest. Sometimes you just need someone to look at your setup, answer your questions honestly, and help you decide what to focus on. I offer focused conversations and practical advice on web security, secure development, and what to watch out for as you build.
hero.outcomeText: A clearer head about your risks, straight answers to the questions you actually have, and a short list of what's worth doing next.

covers.title: What we can cover
items: A look at your current setup and where the real risks are
Honest answers to your security questions without upselling
Practical advice on web security and secure development
What to watch out for as you build and grow
Help prioritizing, so you spend effort where it counts

steps.title: Tell me what you're working on
steps.text: We start with your product, your stack, and what's actually worrying you. No rigid checklist.

steps.title: Talk it through
steps.text: A focused conversation where I answer your questions honestly and point out what I'd pay attention to.

steps.title: Leave with a plan
steps.text: You walk away with clear, practical next steps you can act on at your own pace.

bestFor.title: Best for
items: Early-stage teams figuring out their security basics
Solo developers who want someone to sanity-check their thinking
Small businesses moving things online for the first time
Anyone who needs guidance more than a full engagement

deliverables.title: What you get
items: Focused conversations tailored to your product
Honest, jargon-free answers to your questions
Practical recommendations you can actually apply
A prioritized sense of what to tackle first

cta.title: Not sure what you actually need?
cta.text: If you'd rather talk it through than commit to a full engagement, let's have a conversation.

metadata.title: About | niwo
metadata.description: About Nikita, the person behind niwo, working across security, AI, systems, and product design.

About.title: The person behind niwo systems
About.intro: I'm Nikita, a Cybersecurity student at IU International University with a background in electrical engineering and technical project coordination. I started niwo systems to combine my hands-on learning with real-world practice. Most of what I know comes from building things, breaking them in controlled lab environments, and figuring out why they broke. I work best with developers and small teams who want honest, practical security support, not fear-driven upselling or reports full of jargon nobody reads. If you have a question, a project, or just want to understand your risk better, get in touch.
About.signalText: I care about whether something actually holds up - whether it feels trustworthy, makes sense, and works the way it should when it matters.

drives.title: Mostly just curious about how things work.
drives.text: I like following a system far enough that it starts showing its edges: where it breaks, where it gets fragile, where the assumptions stop being theoretical. That's usually where it gets interesting.

building.title: Figuring out where security, AI, and design overlap.
building.text: That's what I've been spending most of my time on lately. It's the kind of space where you can't get away with surface-level answers. You have to think about whether something actually works in practice, not just in theory. I've also been exploring agent-based workflows with tools like OpenClaw and Codex, trying to understand how software, automation, and reasoning can fit together in ways that are actually useful.

outside.title: I tend to notice the small things.
outside.text: Things like clarity, pacing, and friction stand out to me because they're usually what decides whether something feels intuitive or just frustrating. I like clean interfaces, but only when they actually make something easier to use, not just nicer to look at.

metadata.title: Imprint | niwo systems
metadata.description: Legal notice for niwo systems. Non-commercial informational website. Professional offers handled privately, outside this platform.

provider.title: 1. Provider Identification
provider.body: Responsible according to Section 5 TMG / EU eCommerce Directive

nature.title: 2. Nature of the Website
nature.body: This website is a non-commercial informational project focused on technology and security research. No goods or services are sold directly through this platform.
nature.disclaimerText: This website does not directly offer paid services. Any professional collaboration or offer resulting from private contact is handled outside this platform, for example by email or a separate contract.

contact.title: 3. Contact
contact.body: For legal notices or formal correspondence, please reach out via legal@niwosystems.com. If a physical mailing address is required for formal service, it will be provided upon request.

liability.title: 4. Liability for Content
liability.body: Content is created with care; however, no warranty is given for completeness or accuracy. Links to external sites are provided for convenience, and responsibility for their content lies with the respective providers.

metadata.title: Vulnerability Disclosure Policy | niwo
metadata.description: How to responsibly report vulnerabilities to the niwo security team.

Vdp.title: Vulnerability Disclosure Policy
Vdp.description: Responsible disclosure guidance for researchers reporting potential security issues.

overview.title: 1. Overview
overview.body: Security is a top priority for us at niwo. We appreciate the work of security researchers and the responsible disclosure of potential vulnerabilities.

scope.title: 2. Scope
scope.body: This policy applies to digital assets owned, operated, or maintained by niwo, including niwosystems.com and associated subdomains.

guidelines.title: 3. Guidelines
items: Do not exploit vulnerabilities beyond what is necessary to demonstrate them.
Do not access or modify user data without consent.
Do not disrupt or degrade our services.
Provide sufficient detail so we can reproduce and validate the issue.

reporting.title: 4. Reporting
reporting.body: If you discover a vulnerability, please report it responsibly by emailing security@niwosystems.com.
reporting.calloutTitle: Include the following information where possible:
items: Description of the vulnerability
Steps to reproduce
Potential impact
Suggested remediation, if available

acknowledgement.title: 5. Acknowledgement
acknowledgement.body: We may publicly acknowledge valid reports that help us improve our security posture. Thank you for helping keep niwo and its users safe.

metadata.title: Privacy Policy | niwo
metadata.description: Privacy policy for this website covering email contact, hosting, and locally hosted fonts.

intro.title: 1. Introduction
intro.body: This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect personal data when you visit this website or contact us by email. We aim to handle information responsibly and transparently in line with applicable data protection law, including the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

controller.title: 2. Data Controller
controller.body: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or your personal data, you can contact us using the details above.

dataWeCollect.title: 3. Data We Collect
dataWeCollect.voluntaryIntro: When you contact us by email, we may process:
voluntaryItems: Your name or signature, if you include it
Your company name, phone number, or other contact details, if you include them
The content of your message and any attachments you send
dataWeCollect.voluntaryOutro: This information is used solely to review and respond to your inquiry.
dataWeCollect.automaticBody: When you visit the website, basic technical data may be processed automatically by hosting infrastructure, such as your IP address, request metadata, browser information, and access logs needed for secure delivery of the site.

purposeLegalBasis.title: 4. Purpose & Legal Basis
purposeLegalBasis.body: We process personal data to operate the website securely, respond to inquiries, and protect our systems against abuse or misuse. Depending on context, the legal basis is either our legitimate interests under Article 6(1)(f) GDPR or steps taken at your request prior to entering into a contract under Article 6(1)(b) GDPR.

storageRetention.title: 5. Data Storage & Retention
storageRetention.body: We retain personal data only for as long as necessary to fulfill the purpose for which it was collected, to communicate with you, or to comply with legal obligations. Email correspondence may be retained where needed to document communication or handle follow-up requests.

thirdParties.title: 6. Use of Third-Party Services
thirdParties.hostingBody: This website is hosted using AWS infrastructure. Hosting providers may process technical connection data and server logs as required to deliver the website securely and reliably.
thirdParties.fontsBody: The website uses locally hosted fonts. No font requests are sent to third-party font providers when loading the site.

sharing.title: 7. Data Sharing
sharing.body: We do not sell personal data. Data is only shared where necessary to operate the website, comply with legal obligations, or protect our rights and systems.

rights.title: 8. Your GDPR Rights
rights.intro: Under the GDPR, you may have the right to:
items: Request access to your personal data
Request correction or deletion of your data
Request restriction of processing
Object to processing in certain circumstances
Lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority
rights.outro: To exercise these rights, contact legal@niwosystems.com.

security.title: 9. Data Security
security.body: We take reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect personal data against unauthorized access, loss, misuse, or disclosure. However, no internet transmission or storage system can be guaranteed to be completely secure.

cookies.title: 10. Cookies & Tracking
cookies.body: This website currently does not rely on non-essential tracking cookies or invasive analytics tooling. If this changes in the future, this policy will be updated accordingly.

changes.title: 11. Changes to This Policy
changes.body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect legal, technical, or operational changes. The latest version will always be published on this page.

contact.title: 12. Contact
contact.body: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or about how your personal data is handled, contact legal@niwosystems.com.
